title: Mathematical Foundations for Graphics Engineering
description: This module establishes the complete mathematical toolkit required to understand 3D graphics from first principles. It covers scalar algebra, vector geometry, matrix transformations, quaternion rotations, and analytic geometry — the five pillars that every rendering algorithm rests upon. Students are assumed to have high-school mathematics; no prior linear algebra is expected. All mathematical concepts are grounded in concrete graphics examples drawn from real engine code rather than abstract proofs.
